Application Name: HABISASK - Hunting, Angling and Biodiversity Information
Who was involved: Ryan Brittner, Stuart Holtby, Leanne Silzer, Dalton Stevens
T1 - Platform / Product Footprint: 3 - Virtualized N-Tier: Standard Virtual Machines (Infrastructure as a Service); manual scaling.
T2 - Application Development Platform: 2 - Aging / Limited Modernization: Behind current standards (6 to 10 years old).
T3 - Platform Portability: 3 - Lift & Shift Only: Virtualizable, but requires same OS/Network config (no refactoring).
T4 - Configurability & Extensibility: 3 - Moderate Customization: Some admin settings available; core logic requires developer.
T5 - Support for Modern UX: 3 - Browser (Non-Mobile): Browser-based but not responsive; difficult on tablets.
T6 - Security Controls: 3 - Basic / Compliant: Security Controls met with manual effort.
T7 - Security Controls for Data Sensitiv: 5 - Implemented: All controls for data sensitivity have been implemented.
T8 - Identity Assurance: 4 - Corporate Single Sign-On: Integrated with Corporate Identity Provider (SSO); standard policies apply.
T9 - Resilience & Recovery: 3 - Plan < 48 hrs: Documented Disaster Recovery plan exists; backups valid; not regularly tested. Recovery within 48 hours.
T10 - Observability & Manageability: 4 - Strong: Continuous monitoring, Detailed logs, and basic automation through scripts for some components.
T11 - Vendor and Support Availability: 3 - Basic Support: Internal team supports it best-effort; documentation is patchy.
T12 - Integration Capabilities: 3 - Monitored Legacy: Scripts or batch jobs with error logging; alerts IT on failure.
T13 - Integrations: 4 - 1-10 Integrations: Low complexity; clear data flow diagrams exist.
T14 - Data Accessibility: 4 - Has ETL Capabilities: Users have access to the Extract, Transform, Load capability.
Notes:
Danny McLeod is already working on a new version of this so we can update the underlying infrastructure.  

GeoCortex that we are running is 4.15 which has a end of life of June 2028.   The company has been bought by VertaGIS which is working on replacing GeoCortex with Verta GIS Studio 5.
